name: Thai Green Curry Chicken
steps: Cook rice per package directions.
Simmer curry paste in coconut milk 2 min to bloom.
Add chicken and bell pepper, simmer 10-12 min until cooked through.
Stir in fish sauce and serve over rice.
steps_detailed: Pound or blend the lemongrass, galangal, and half the kaffir lime leaf with the chili powder into a rough paste.
Cook the jasmine rice separately, 15-18 minutes.
Fry the paste in a little of the coconut milk over medium heat, 2-3 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the chicken and shallot, and cook 5-6 minutes.
Add the remaining coconut milk, the pepper, and the remaining kaffir lime leaf, and simmer 8-10 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the fish sauce and finish with the basil.
Serve over the rice.
description: Thai green curry gets its color and aromatic depth from a paste of green chilies, lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaf pounded together - a jarred generic curry paste misses that fresh, bright aromatic hit, and basmati isn't the rice this dish actually wants.

name: Shrimp Pad Thai
steps: Soak rice noodles in hot water until pliable, drain.
Scramble egg in a hot wok, set aside.
Stir-fry shrimp and carrot 3-4 min, add noodles.
Whisk fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, and peanut butter; toss everything together with the egg.
steps_detailed: Soak the rice noodles in warm water for 20-25 minutes until pliable but still firm (they'll finish cooking in the pan), then drain.
Whisk the fish sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, and peanut butter together for the sauce.
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat. Push everything to one side as you go so each addition sears rather than steams.
Cook the shrimp 1-2 minutes per side until pink, then push to the side.
Pour the beaten eggs into the cleared space and scramble quickly, 30-45 seconds.
Add the garlic and noodles, then pour the sauce over everything and toss vigorously to coat, 2-3 minutes.
Add the carrot and bean sprouts in the last minute so they stay crisp.
Top with scallions before serving.
description: Pad Thai, stir-fried rice noodles with a sweet-sour-savory sauce, is Thailand's most internationally famous dish.

name: Thai Basil Beef
steps: Cook rice per package directions.
Stir-fry garlic and beef over high heat 2-3 min.
Add bell pepper, fish sauce, and soy sauce, cook 2 min more.
Remove from heat and stir in basil. Serve over rice.
steps_detailed: Cook the rice per package directions.
Whisk the fish sauce, soy sauce, and sriracha together.

Heat a wok or skillet over high heat until just starting to smoke. Add the beef in a single layer and let it sear undisturbed 30-45 seconds before stirring — this is what gives stir-fried beef good char instead of a gray, steamed texture.
Add the garlic and bell pepper, stir-frying 2 minutes until the pepper is just tender-crisp.
Pour in the sauce and toss to coat, cooking 1 more minute.
Remove from heat and stir in the basil until just wilted. Serve over rice.
description: A beef stir-fry built on Thai basil's distinctive anise-like flavor, sharper than Italian basil.

name: Coconut Shrimp Soup
steps: Cook rice per package directions.
Simmer coconut milk with ginger 3-4 min.
Add shrimp, simmer 4-5 min until pink.
Stir in fish sauce and lime juice, serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Cook the rice per package directions.
Simmer the broth, coconut milk, and ginger together for 5 minutes to infuse the ginger's flavor.
Add the shrimp and fish sauce, and simmer 3-4 minutes until the shrimp are pink and cooked through.
Remove from heat and stir in the lime juice — adding acid at the end keeps it bright rather than muted from prolonged cooking.
Top with cilantro and serve over or alongside rice.
description: Tom kha-style, a coconut milk soup - real versions lean on galangal and lemongrass for their aromatic backbone.

name: Thai Peanut Chicken Noodles
steps: Cook noodles per package directions.
Stir-fry chicken until cooked through, 5-6 min.
Whisk peanut butter, soy sauce, and lime juice; toss with noodles, chicken, and carrot.
steps_detailed: Soak the rice noodles in warm water 20-25 minutes until pliable, then drain.
Whisk the peanut butter, soy sauce, and lime juice with 2-3 tablespoons warm water until smooth and pourable.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken with garlic and ginger, 5-6 minutes, until cooked through.
Add the noodles and carrot, then pour the peanut sauce over everything.
Toss until evenly coated and heated through, about 2 minutes.
description: A simple peanut-sauce noodle dish, common in Thai-American cooking.

name: Thai Basil Chicken (Pad Krapow)
steps: Cook rice per package directions.
Brown chicken, stir in fish sauce and sriracha.
Fold in basil off heat, serve over rice.
steps_detailed: Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat a wok or skillet over high heat. Add the garlic and cook 15-20 seconds until fragrant, then add the ground chicken, breaking it into small pieces.
Cook 5-6 minutes, stirring often, until browned and cooked through.
Add the fish sauce, soy sauce, and sriracha, tossing to coat.
Remove from heat and stir in the basil until just wilted.
Fry an egg separately, sunny-side up, and serve on top of the chicken and rice — the runny yolk is meant to mix through as you eat.
description: Pad krapow, stir-fried meat with Thai holy basil and chilies over rice, one of Thailand's most common everyday dishes.

name: Thai Shrimp Curry
steps: Simmer curry paste with coconut milk 2 min.
Add shrimp and bell pepper, simmer 6-8 min.
Serve over rice.
steps_detailed: Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat a spoonful of the thick cream from the top of the coconut milk can in a pan over medium heat. Fry the curry paste 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the remaining coconut milk and fish sauce, bring to a simmer.
Add the bell pepper and simmer 5 minutes until starting to soften.
Add the shrimp and cook 3-4 minutes until pink and cooked through — don't overcook, shrimp go rubbery fast.
Stir in basil at the end and serve over rice.
description: A coconut-based shrimp curry, built on the same aromatic curry paste base as green curry.

name: Thai Beef Salad (Larb-style)
steps: Brown beef, drain excess fat.
Toss with lime juice, fish sauce, and mint.
Serve in lettuce leaves.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef in a dry skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it into small crumbles, about 6-7 minutes, until cooked through with a bit of char.
Remove from heat and let cool for a minute or two — adding the herbs and lime while it's too hot wilts them too aggressively.
Toss the beef with lime juice, fish sauce, sriracha, red onion, mint, and cilantro.
Taste and adjust — larb should be bright, tangy, and a little spicy. Serve piled into lettuce leaves.
description: A Thai take on the wider Southeast Asian larb tradition of meat tossed with lime, fish sauce, and toasted rice powder.

name: Thai Peanut Chicken Skewers
steps: Whisk peanut butter, coconut aminos, and lime juice.
Skewer chicken, grill 10-12 min, brushing with sauce.
steps_detailed: Whisk the peanut butter, coconut aminos, lime juice, garlic, and ginger together with a splash of water to loosen it into a marinade.
Toss the chicken cubes in the marinade and let sit at least 20 minutes (or up to a few hours in the fridge).
Thread onto skewers if using.
Grill or broil 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until charred in spots and cooked through (165F).
description: Grilled chicken skewers with peanut sauce, a common Thai street food.

name: Thai Chicken Larb Jar
steps: Toss chicken with lime juice and fish sauce.
Layer over rice with cucumber in a jar.
steps_detailed: Toss the cooked chicken with lime juice, fish sauce, red onion, and cilantro.
Layer the rice, cucumber, and dressed chicken in a jar.
description: A portable version of larb, meat tossed with lime and fish sauce.

name: Thai Peanut Noodle Salad
steps: Whisk peanut butter and lime juice with 2 tbsp warm water.
Toss with noodles, carrot, and cucumber.
steps_detailed: Whisk the peanut butter, lime juice, and soy sauce with 2-3 tablespoons warm water until smooth.
Toss the cooled noodles with the sauce, carrot, cucumber, and scallions.
description: A simple peanut-dressed noodle salad.

name: Thai Shrimp Salad Jar
steps: Toss shrimp with lime juice and mint.
Layer over rice with cucumber.
steps_detailed: Toss the shrimp with lime juice, fish sauce, and mint.
Layer the rice, cucumber, and dressed shrimp in a jar.
description: A portable shrimp salad, lime and fish sauce giving it a Thai character.

name: Thai Coconut Rice Porridge
steps: Simmer the rice in the coconut milk and water for 25-30 minutes until thick.

ingredients_full.note: leaf, torn, standing in for fresh

steps_detailed: Simmer the jasmine rice in the coconut milk and 0.75 cup water with the torn lime leaf over low heat, stirring often, 30-35 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Remove the lime leaf, stir in the honey.
The lime leaf's floral perfume, even in this small quantity, is a genuinely Thai touch that plain coconut rice porridge elsewhere in the region typically skips.
description: A simple coconut rice porridge.

name: Thai Cucumber Salad Snack
steps: Toss cucumber with lime juice and honey.
steps_detailed: Whisk the lime juice and honey together.
Toss with the cucumber and red onion. Let sit 5-10 minutes before serving for the flavors to meld.
description: A simple, cooling cucumber salad.

name: Thai Roasted Cashews
steps: Toss cashews with sriracha.
Roast at 350F for 6-8 min.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 350F.
Toss the cashews with sriracha and honey until coated.
Spread on a lined baking sheet and roast 8-10 minutes, checking at 6, until fragrant and slightly darkened.
Let cool completely before eating — they'll crisp up as they cool.
description: A simple roasted cashew snack.

name: Thai Mango Sticky Rice
steps: Cook rice per package directions, stir in half the coconut milk.
Top with mango and remaining coconut milk.
steps_detailed: Cook the rice per package directions, using half water and half coconut milk instead of all water for extra richness.
While the rice is still warm, stir in the remaining coconut milk and honey.
Let sit 5 minutes to absorb, then serve topped with the mango.
description: Khao niew mamuang, sweet sticky rice with ripe mango, one of Thailand's most beloved desserts.

name: Thai Iced Tea Chia Pudding
steps: Whisk chia seeds into coconut milk and honey.
Chill at least 3 hours until set.
steps_detailed: Whisk the chia seeds into the coconut milk, honey, and cinnamon, making sure there are no clumps.
Let sit 5 minutes, then whisk again to break up any seeds that settled.
Chill at least 2 hours, or overnight, until thickened to a pudding consistency.
description: A modern fusion dessert built on the flavor of Thai iced tea.

name: Thai Peanut Dipping Sauce
steps: Whisk all ingredients with 2-3 tbsp warm water until smooth.
steps_detailed: Whisk all ingredients together with 2-3 tablespoons warm water, adding water gradually until it reaches a smooth, pourable consistency.
description: A simple peanut sauce, common alongside grilled meats and noodle dishes.

name: Nam Prik (Thai Chili Dip)
steps: Whisk all ingredients together.
steps_detailed: Whisk all ingredients together until the honey is fully dissolved.
Taste and adjust — nam prik should be intensely salty, sour, and spicy all at once. Serve alongside grilled meats or vegetables.
description: Nam prik, a broad category of Thai chili-based dips, endlessly varied by region and household.

name: Thai Turkey Basil Lettuce Cups
steps: Toss turkey with basil and fish sauce.
Serve in lettuce leaves.
steps_detailed: Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the garlic and cook 15-20 seconds, then add the turkey, breaking it apart.
Cook 6-7 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Stir in fish sauce and sriracha, then remove from heat and fold in the basil until just wilted.
Spoon into lettuce leaves to serve.
description: A lighter, ground-turkey take on pad krapow's basil-chili flavor profile.

name: Thai Mango Salad
steps: Whisk lime juice and peanut butter for the dressing.
Toss with mango, carrot, and mint.
steps_detailed: Whisk the lime juice and peanut butter with a splash of warm water for the dressing.
Toss with mango, carrot, red onion, and mint.
description: A simple, bright mango salad.

name: Tom Yum Shrimp Soup
steps: Simmer broth with sriracha 3-4 min.
Add shrimp, cook until pink, stir in lime juice and cilantro.

ingredients_full.note: smashed and cut into short pieces

steps_detailed: Simmer the broth with the lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaf for 8-10 minutes to infuse.
Add the shrimp and jalapeno, and simmer 3-4 minutes until the shrimp are just pink.
Stir in the lime juice and fish sauce off the heat - tom yum's sourness should hit right away, so add these at the very end rather than simmering them.
Top with the cilantro.
The lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaf pieces are meant to be pushed aside at the table, not eaten whole.
description: Tom yum's whole identity comes from lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaf simmered directly in the broth, with fresh chilies for heat - sriracha and ordinary ginger are a completely different, flatter direction than the sharp, citrusy sourness real tom yum is known for.
